_note: Current operational context / open items captured from Kendall's texts, calls, and email that aren't movements (events.json) or invoices. The audit surfaces these so decisions made off-spine don't fall through the cracks. Update as items resolve; remove when closed.

items.title: STUCK 6/15-17 run — RESOLVED 6/22: sprinter via Jeremy (FFE), PICKED UP, delivering to Brooklyn (Carriacou) WEDNESDAY 6/24
items.detail: RESOLVED 6/22. The 6/15-17 run (3 measured pallets: BUF 52in/1,111 lb · GR 35in/713 lb · FO 59in/1,211 lb, ~3,035 lb) was the relief valve for critically-low inventory. Jeremy (FFE) sourced a reefer SPRINTER (~$4,000), PICKED UP 6/22, delivering to BROOKLYN — Carriacou Logistics (Scholes St; contact Mendez 347-248-7056, Curlon & Mohamed on dock) — WEDNESDAY 6/24. This is the S3/best-case outcome: lands in time for this week's store deliveries + UNFI (6/23-25) + Fancy Food (6/26-30). Why the sprinter premium was worth it: Edison was down to just BUF 22/FO 33/GR 15 (Lucy 6/22) and nothing in Brooklyn — Edison alone could NOT cover this week, so Wednesday delivery was the only option that lands product in time. ON ARRIVAL convert to inventory (location = Brooklyn/Carriacou) and tell Lucy which stops pull from Brooklyn vs Edison's remaining 70 cs.
items.owner: Kendall / Claude — log receipt at Brooklyn on Wed arrival; coordinate Localee pulls Edison vs Brooklyn

items.title: Carriacou (Brooklyn) warehouse — NEW storage option; Mendez holding space, needs date/time/# pallets (BLOCKED on securing a truck)
items.detail: Kendall reached out to Carriacou (Brooklyn; contact Mendez) about storing product there — NOT previously used. NEXT: once a truck is secured (see the freight items above), send Mendez the date/time/pallets to lock the Brooklyn receiving. Jake also coordinated with Mendez. SPECS NOW KNOWN (John, 6/19): 3 pallets — P1 48x40x54 1111lb, P2 48x40x35 713lb, P3 48x40x59 1211lb. So #pallets (3) + dims are answered; only the truck + date/time remain.

items.detail: Yuliana (6/19): ice packs were still cold but slid to one side and there weren't enough, so the dips were never cold on arrival. A DTC shipping cold-chain failure. ACTION: decide on a make-good (refund/replacement) and fix the DTC pack-out (more/secured ice packs).
